---
name: Spec / architecture question
about: A question about why Garrison is designed the way it is, or whether a spec says what it should.
title: "[question] "
labels: question
---

## What you're asking

<!-- The question, in one or two sentences. -->

## Where you already looked

Please confirm you've checked the binding documents before asking.
Which did you look at?

- [ ] `RATIONALE.md` — the 12 architectural decisions with
  alternatives considered.
- [ ] `ARCHITECTURE.md` — system components, data model, event
  flow, build plan.
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` — scope discipline, locked dependency list,
  concurrency rules.
- [ ] The active milestone context at
  `specs/_context/m{N}-context.md`.
- [ ] The active milestone spec / plan / tasks in
  `specs/m{N}-*/`.
- [ ] The most recent milestone retro at `docs/retros/m{N}.md`.

Most "wouldn't it be better if..." questions are already answered in
`RATIONALE.md`. Most "does the supervisor do X?" questions are
answered in the relevant spec. If you've already checked and still
have the question, that's exactly what this template is for.

## Your current understanding

<!--
What do you currently think the answer is? Describe your mental
model so the response can correct it precisely. This is much more
useful than "please explain X" — we already have the docs for that.
-->

## The specific gap

<!--
What did the docs not tell you? "RATIONALE.md §3 says X but I
don't see how that's compatible with Y from ARCHITECTURE.md"
is the kind of question that produces useful corrections to the
docs, not just an answer.
-->
